role_title: Product Manager - Client Experience Pathways, VP II - State Street Investment Management

level_signals: VP II title signals senior individual contributor
Comp range $110,000–$188,750 consistent with senior IC at large financial institution

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Understanding of back-office, mid-office systems and workflows
Deep understanding of asset management and client-experience ecosystems
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint
Experience managing a product or application through its lifecycle
Hands‑on experience working with technology, design, data, and QA teams
Strong business acumen with the ability to engage with Business SMEs, Engineers, and Architects
Experience with Atlassian products (Jira, Confluence) for Agile project management and collaboration
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and strategic thinking skills
Bachelor's degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience supporting senior leaders within Product, Investment Services, or adjacent financial services functions a plus
Bachelor's degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred

scope.surface: Client Experience ecosystem platforms (front, middle, and back-office integration) aligned to the Alpha target operating model
core_responsibilities: Own product leadership for current-state Middle Office integration with Client Experience platforms, coordinating performance, accounting, security master, and index data flows across the ecosystem
Understand the Alpha Target State and ensure that Client Experience features, reporting and platform experiences reinforce that vision
Understand client's current state operating model across front, middle, and back-office capabilities and identify where they diverge from the Alpha target state and product roadmap
Support OKR definition, tracking, and reporting, ensuring alignment between strategy, execution, and outcomes
Prioritize initiatives to maximize value, balance impact, risk, effort, and dependencies
Drive adoption, roll-out and continuous improvement post-launch
Foster innovation by embracing and leveraging effective use of the latest AI tools
Drive cross-functional alignment across business teams, technology, and operations
Translate strategy into epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria
Evaluate and integrate third-party tools and frameworks that enhance platform capabilities and reduce time-to-market
Identify and shape meaningful AI use cases that enhance how Program Managers plan, execute, and manage delivery
Ensure operational excellence and strong risk-management practices

green_flags: Clearly defined product surface (Client Experience / Alpha operating model)
Comprehensive benefits package described including 401K match, medical, dental, vision
Company explicitly describes inclusive development opportunities and flexible work-life support
red_flags: Location listed as Boston, Massachusetts (onsite implied) but remote eligibility not stated — fully remote candidates may be ineligible
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
Wide salary range ($78,750 spread) may indicate significant variance in seniority expectations or location-based adjustments
JD references proprietary 'Alpha target operating model' — candidates unfamiliar with State Street's internal platform architecture may face a steep learning curve

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (inferred as onsite from city listing and no remote language in body)
seniority.level (inferred from VP II title, years requirements, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred false; no direct reports mentioned)

scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as metrics)
compensation.location_pay_differential (stated indirectly: 'If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ')
